whyUpgrade.headline: Why upgrade equipment — efficiency, durability, and lifecycle value
points: The best specialist balances efficiency, durability, maintainability, and lifecycle cost — not lowest kWh alone.
UK ETL products are independently verified — typically top 25% in class — reducing procurement risk.
Lifetime cost beats sticker price: energy, maintenance, and downtime drive real payback.
Fit for purpose matters — capacity, duty cycle, controls, and service access determine lasting performance.
Building fabric sets baseload — insulation before oversized cookline or HVAC capex.
Trajectory and savings projection show medium-term € impact when replacing specific equipment lines.

buildingRetrofitBenefits.headline: Sustainable renovations — business case for buildings
buildingRetrofitBenefits.summary: Green retrofits raise property value, unlock grants, cut operating costs, and improve climate resilience — not only environmental benefit.
themes: Fabric and insulation lower baseload before oversized equipment capex.
Climate-proofing (flood, heat, storm) protects asset value alongside kWh savings.
Stack building measures with ETL equipment swaps for synergistic payback.
Confirm grant eligibility with Andrieus — regions and property type matter.

restaurantDesign.headline: Restaurant design that pays for itself
restaurantDesign.summary: Hospitality buildings often use 5-7× more energy per m² than other commercial stock — smart layout and ventilation design can cut costs while improving service flow.
zones: Separate hot and cold kitchen zones — refrigeration away from cookline cuts compressor load up to ~25%.
Demand-controlled ventilation tracks cooking activity instead of running flat-out overnight.
Strategic equipment placement shortens prep paths and reduces idle runtime in off-peak hours.
